title: Paul Saladino MD: What I eat in a day

chunks.title: Introduction and Morning Routine
chunks.summary: Paul Saladino introduces himself and shares his morning routine, which includes surfing and consuming raw organic honey and reverse osmosis water. This segment sets the stage for discussing his daily nutrition practices.
chunks.transcript:
my name is Paul saladino I'm a double board 
certified medical doctor I'm going to show you   what I eat in a day I start my mornings early 
I like to get up and surf I'll go outside put   my feet in the grass get sun on my face and

organic honey I like this honey because it's   local it's from a farm it's very high up in the 
mountains so I know it's glyphosate free I have   a couple of spoonfuls of this before I get out 
to Surf I have some reverse osmosis water and   then it's off to the ocean for a couple of hours 
of Surfing every morning usually the first thing

chunks.title: Breakfast and First Meal
chunks.summary: In this segment, Saladino details his breakfast, which consists of a smoothie made from various organic fruits and raw cow's milk kefir, followed by a meal rich in meat and organs. He emphasizes the importance of sourcing high-quality, glyphosate-free meat from local farms.

chunks.title: Afternoon Snack and Dinner Introduction
chunks.summary: Saladino discusses his afternoon snack of local organic pineapple and transitions into preparing for dinner. He shares his preference for ground beef, occasionally enjoying a ribeye steak during the weekends.

chunks.summary: He describes the components of his dinner, which includes grass-fed ribeye steak, raw butter, and plantains. He also emphasizes the use of organ supplements to ensure nutritional completeness.

chunks.title: Benefits of Raw Dairy
chunks.summary: Saladino explains the benefits of consuming raw dairy products, citing their nutritional richness and potential health advantages over heated dairy. He provides insights into the relationship between raw dairy and reduced allergies.
chunks.transcript:

chunks.title: Carbohydrates and Food Choices
chunks.summary: He discusses his carbohydrate sources, advocating for fruit over grains and beans due to their toxicity and nutritional drawbacks. Saladino believes fruit is the least toxic carbohydrate source.

chunks.title: Caloric Intake and Macronutrient Breakdown
chunks.summary: Saladino shares an overview of his daily caloric intake and macronutrient breakdown, consisting of significant protein, carbohydrates, and fat amounts. He notes the importance of adjusting macronutrients based on individual activity levels.

chunks.title: Eating Schedule and Fasting Insights
chunks.summary: He concludes with insights into his eating schedule, advocating for a late meal finish to optimize sleep and performance. Paul explains that he is not strict about intermittent fasting but values the timing of his meals.
chunks.transcript:

chunks.title: Closing Remarks and Supplement Promotion
chunks.summary: In the closing segment, Saladino encourages viewers to try organ supplements and shares resources for calculating macronutrients. He expresses gratitude for viewer engagement and emphasizes the importance of nutrient-rich organ foods.
